channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UCLA Wins First NCAA Women's Basketball Championship With Decisive Victory Over South Carolina
item.description: UCLA captured its first NCAA women's basketball championship with a commanding 79-51 victory over South Carolina on April 5, 2026. Gabriela Jaquez led the Bruins with 21 points, adding 10 rebounds and...

item.title: Fuel Supply Issues Threaten Canberra's Recent Price Drop
item.description: Canberra residents enjoyed a brief respite from high petrol prices recently, but the relief may be short-lived. While prices dipped significantly, concerns are growing regarding ongoing supply chain i...

item.title: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen engages with youth during Artemis II mission Q&A
item.description: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen, currently aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft as part of the Artemis II mission, took part in a live Q&A session with Canadian children from space. Hansen, who is set to b...

item.title: Eldoret Residents Grapple With Growing Water Shortage Despite Major Tariff Increase
item.description: Residents of Eldoret town in Uasin Gishu County are facing a worsening water shortage despite the local water company increasing tariffs by 300 percent in a bid to improve service delivery. Many neigh...

item.title: Transport Minister Appeals for Road Safety During Easter Holidays
item.description: Minister of Transport Barbara Creecy has called on all South Africans to act responsibly on the roads during the Easter holidays. Increased traffic on major routes is expected as travellers head to re...

item.title: Alberta rural leaders seek regulated grizzly hunting amid rising bear conflicts
item.description: Rural municipalities across Alberta, led by Cardston County and represented by the Rural Municipalities of Alberta (RMA), are urging the provincial government to reinstate a regulated grizzly bear hun...

item.title: Researchers Develop Ultrathin Layer to Efficiently Trap and Convert Light
item.description: Researchers at the University of Warsaw, in collaboration with several Polish institutions, have engineered a nanoscale structure capable of trapping infrared light in a layer just 40 nanometers thick...

item.title: PNP-ACG records 332 arrests linked to cybercrime operations in early 2026
item.description: The Philippine National Police Anti-Cybercrime Group (PNP-ACG) has reported that it arrested 332 individuals for different types of cybercrime during the first quarter of 2026. In its weekend statemen...

item.title: Karachi matric exams rescheduled as board shifts to digital system
item.description: The Board of Secondary Education Karachi (BSEK) has announced a rescheduling of the Secondary School Certificate (SSC) exams in Karachi. Originally planned to begin on 7 April and run until 2 May, the...

item.title: Trump issues strong warning to Iran amid rising Strait of Hormuz tensions
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a stern warning to Iran, vowing to bring 'hell' upon the country if it disrupts or threatens navigation in the vital Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s ...

item.title: Labour Government Implements Key Measures to Support Workers, Pensioners and Families
item.description: The UK government under Prime Minister Keir Starmer has introduced significant reforms aimed at improving the lives of workers, pensioners, and children. From Monday, employees will benefit from day-o...

item.title: Reflecting on South Africa’s Resilience and Freedom as Easter and Freedom Month Arrive
item.description: The article, published by SAnews.gov.za during the Easter holidays of 2026, serves as an opinion piece encouraging South Africans to pause and reflect on the country's progress and challenges. It begi...

item.title: NDPC investigates Remita and Sterling Bank for potential data breach
item.description: The Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) don start investigation into alleged data breach wey involve Remita Payment Services Ltd., Sterling Bank and some other organisations. Reports say say som...

item.title: Major sponsors withdraw from Wireless Festival following controversy over Kanye West headlining
item.description: Two major global brands, Pepsi and Diageo, have pulled their sponsorship of the Wireless Festival after the announcement that Kanye West, also known as Ye, would headline the event this July in London...

item.title: DRC to start receiving deportees under US-funded arrangement
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has confirmed that it will start receiving deportees under a newly established deal with the United States, commencing this month. The Congolese government has n...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard rescues passengers and crew from grounded vessel in Lubang, Occidental Mindoro
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) successfully assisted 162 passengers and eight crew members of the MV Our Lady of Fatima 03, a passenger ship that ran aground near Lubang, Occidental Mindoro. The inc...

item.title: Experiencing the Porsche 911 GT3 from the passenger seat at the Australian Grand Prix
item.description: Motoring journalist Tony Davis recounts an exhilarating experience at the 2026 Australian Grand Prix in Melbourne, where he had the rare chance to enjoy high-speed laps as a passenger in the latest Po...

item.title: US President Considers Cabinet Changes Amid Iran War Fallout
item.description: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump is reportedly contemplating a broader shake-up of his cabinet following the recent removal of Attorney General Pam Bondi. Sources familiar with White House discussio...

item.title: Nadra official and Afghan nationals held in Karachi over fake CNICs case
item.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has detained a National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra) officer allegedly linked to the illegal issuance of Pakistani identity cards to Afghan nation...

item.title: Army frees 31 kidnapped worshippers after Easter attack in Kaduna
